title: CAWS Policy
description: Strict policy schema for CAWS vNext. Single authority for risk-tier budgets, gate modes, waivers, and scope-passthrough rules. Rejects: label on tier objects, unknown top-level fields, dangerously broad non_governed_zones patterns without explicit force flag.

version.description: Policy schema version. Pinned to 1 for v1.x.

risk_tiers.description: Per-tier budgets. Tier keys are integer strings '1', '2', '3' — matching spec.risk_tier coerced to string. Tier 1 is most strict.

gates.description: Closed gate vocabulary. Adding a new gate requires a schema bump.

waivers.description: Waiver-related policy rules. Individual waivers live in .caws/waivers/, not here.

default_expiry_days.description: Default validity for newly created waivers if no expires_at supplied.

non_governed_zones.description: Glob patterns whose matching paths skip ALL scope-boundary enforcement. Wins over scope.out. Dangerously broad patterns ('**', '*', '**/*', '.', './', './**', '/**') are rejected unless non_governed_zones_force is explicitly true (enforced via top-level allOf/if).

non_governed_zones_force.description: Override the broad-pattern rejection for non_governed_zones. Authority-relinquishing decision; should require explicit policy review.

root_passthrough.description: The ONLY mechanism by which root-level files (no '/' in path) bypass scope.in. Empty by default — root-level files require explicit listing in either policy.root_passthrough OR a spec's scope.in.

edit_rules.description: Declarative governance rules. Runtime enforcement is policy-derived; these flags document intent.

policy_and_code_same_pr.description: false: policy.yaml changes and code changes must not appear in the same PR.

agents.description: SESSION-OWNERSHIP-METADATA-001 (C1 storage-bounds interpretation): configuration for multi-agent ownership metadata. Optional; consumed by upstream collectors and downstream guards, NOT by the agents.json writer.

last_modified_paths_ttl_seconds.description: Caller-visible TTL (seconds) for filtering last_modified_paths before invoking refreshAgentClaim. Bounds [60, 86400]; default 1800 (30 minutes). The CAWS store writer does NOT consume this value — it stores caller-pruned arrays verbatim subject to structural validation and FIFO cap. TTL enforcement lives in whichever upstream surface assembles the path set.

max_files.description: Maximum files changed in a single PR/slice for this tier.

description.description: Optional human description (e.g. 'Critical', 'Standard'). NOT a 'label' field — that name is reserved-out to surface the legacy drift.

mode.description: Closed mode vocabulary. block = non-zero exit. warn = advisory zero exit. skip = disabled.

thresholds.description: Gate-specific thresholds. Schema is per-gate; validated at gate-implementation time.
